Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group A is protected group no community group and LocalResident is FALSE for all A and Group B has LocalResident TRUE for all and some have community group

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Bias likely in favor of Group B because they get a score adjustment community boost if in Highland Civic Darts Club and in Group A there is no such adjustment

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Systematic differences appear because Group B members with community group get a plus 10 boost potentially turning a borderline score into approval

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For example U003 has preliminary score 695 adjusted to 705 then approved meanwhile in Group A P002 with score 710 is rejected

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That suggests bias against Group A because Group A never gets a community boost while some in Group B do

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Group A is protected group that might mean they are a protected class and the system might be biased against them

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Since Group B is unprotected they have advantage and the bias is in favor of the unprotected group B due to the community boost attribute

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Also note that LocalResident is TRUE for B and FALSE for A that might be another factor perhaps being a local resident gives some advantage

Name: Validation - PE
Text: But no direct adjustment from that only community group membership

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The system systematically disadvantages people who are not local residents because they cannot access the local community group boost

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Answer Group A log exhibits bias because they are denied the community boost that is only available to local residents in Group B and this leads to systematic differences in final decisions
